item.title: ITS-ShipFormer: An Informative Token Selection Former for SAR Ship Recognition
item.dc:creator: Yuanzhe Shang
Wei Pu
Congwen Wu
Yulin Huang
Yin Zhang
Junjie Wu
Jianyu Yang
Jianqi Wu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
item.description: Ship automatic target recognition (ATR) in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images plays a crucial role in maritime domain awareness. However, the sea clutter interference may make the model fail to focus on informative ship target regions for recognition. All existing SAR ship ATR models use the entire SAR ship image as input. But not all regions in SAR ship images contribute positively to recognition. However, the intraclass diversity and interclass similarity make the SAR ship ATR task more challenging. In this study, we propose a novel transformer-based architecture that addresses the two core challenges, named informative token selection former (ITS-ShipFormer). ITS-ShipFormer selects and guides the model’s attention to the informative token of the ship target regions. The ITS-ShipFormer consists of the multihead dynamic local convolution (MHDLC) block in the early stages, transformer blocks equipped with a sea clutter suppression module (SCSM) in the latter stages, and a discriminative hybrid loss. SCSM automatically distinguishes the informative ship tokens and useless sea clutter tokens by two carefully designed strategies. In response to the other challenge, MHDLC is designed to enhance the feature extraction ability, and the hybrid discriminative loss is proposed to add constraints on the CLS token and the informative tokens simultaneously. The experimental results on benchmark datasets OpenSARShip and FUSAR-Ship jointly verify the effectiveness of our design. Different from previous works introducing the transformer structure in ITS-ShipFormer utilizes the overlooked innate and unique advantages of the transformer structure to address our challenge. Published: 2026-04-06T19:57:12+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems Score: 0.848 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yuanzhe Shang; Wei Pu; Congwen Wu; Yulin Huang; Yin Zhang; Junjie Wu; Jianyu Yang; Jianqi Wu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems

DOI: 10.1109/tnnls.2026.3661083

Relevance: 0.848 (must_read)

Ship automatic target recognition (ATR) in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images plays a crucial role in maritime domain awareness. However, the sea clutter interference may make the model fail to focus on informative ship target regions for recognition. All existing SAR ship ATR models use the entire SAR ship image as input. But not all regions in SAR ship images contribute positively to recognition. However, the intraclass diversity and interclass similarity make the SAR ship ATR task more challenging. In this study, we propose a novel transformer-based architecture that addresses the two core challenges, named informative token selection former (ITS-ShipFormer). ITS-ShipFormer selects and guides the model’s attention to the informative token of the ship target regions. The ITS-ShipFormer consists of the multihead dynamic local convolution (MHDLC) block in the early stages, transformer blocks equipped with a sea clutter suppression module (SCSM) in the latter stages, and a discriminative hybrid loss. SCSM automatically distinguishes the informative ship tokens and useless sea clutter tokens by two carefully designed strategies. In response to the other challenge, MHDLC is designed to enhance the feature extraction ability, and the hybrid discriminative loss is proposed to add constraints on the CLS token and the informative tokens simultaneously. The experimental results on benchmark datasets OpenSARShip and FUSAR-Ship jointly verify the effectiveness of our design. Different from previous works introducing the transformer structure in ITS-ShipFormer utilizes the overlooked innate and unique advantages of the transformer structure to address our challenge.

item.title: SAINet: Scale-Adaptive and Information-Enhanced Remote Sensing Cross-Modal Retrieval
item.dc:creator: Jirui Huang
Yaxiong Chen
Shili Xiong
Jie Wen
Xiaoqiang Lu
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: Remote sensing cross-modal retrieval bridges modality discrepancies between visual and textual data, enabling precise information retrieval for advanced remote sensing applications. Unlike natural image-based retrieval, it faces challenges from highly flexible scale variations in remote sensing images and the limited exploitation of modality complementarity. To address these challenges, we propose Scale-Adaptive and Information-enhanced Network (SAINet), a novel framework designed to facilitate multi-scale visual-textual alignment. Our core idea is to learn joint visual-textual representations in a latent space while adaptively selecting the optimal scale for retrieval. This approach balances redundancy reduction at large scales with information retention at small scales. Furthermore, since textual descriptions often fail to fully capture image details, leveraging cross-modal complementarity is essential for improving retrieval performance. To address this, we propose the Scale-Adaptive Optimization Module (SAOM), which dynamically adjusts the scale of image feature learning based on semantic requirements, providing scale-aware supervision to enhance cross-modal alignment. Additionally, we introduce the Semantic Collaboration Consistency Enhancement (SCCE) module, which exploits modality complementarity under visual guidance and preserves textual semantic consistency, thereby improving cross-modal alignment for ambiguous RS captions. We conduct extensive experiments on the RSICD and RSITMD datasets, demonstrating the superior performance of our approach. Published: 2026-04-07T16:28:33+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.845 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jirui Huang; Yaxiong Chen; Shili Xiong; Jie Wen; Xiaoqiang Lu

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104356

Relevance: 0.845 (must_read)

Remote sensing cross-modal retrieval bridges modality discrepancies between visual and textual data, enabling precise information retrieval for advanced remote sensing applications. Unlike natural image-based retrieval, it faces challenges from highly flexible scale variations in remote sensing images and the limited exploitation of modality complementarity. To address these challenges, we propose Scale-Adaptive and Information-enhanced Network (SAINet), a novel framework designed to facilitate multi-scale visual-textual alignment. Our core idea is to learn joint visual-textual representations in a latent space while adaptively selecting the optimal scale for retrieval. This approach balances redundancy reduction at large scales with information retention at small scales. Furthermore, since textual descriptions often fail to fully capture image details, leveraging cross-modal complementarity is essential for improving retrieval performance. To address this, we propose the Scale-Adaptive Optimization Module (SAOM), which dynamically adjusts the scale of image feature learning based on semantic requirements, providing scale-aware supervision to enhance cross-modal alignment. Additionally, we introduce the Semantic Collaboration Consistency Enhancement (SCCE) module, which exploits modality complementarity under visual guidance and preserves textual semantic consistency, thereby improving cross-modal alignment for ambiguous RS captions. We conduct extensive experiments on the RSICD and RSITMD datasets, demonstrating the superior performance of our approach.

item.title: Multimodal Semantic-Scale Network for Remote Sensing Image Classification
item.dc:creator: Caihong Mu
Bokun Ma
Yi Liu
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: Self-attention mechanisms specialize in capturing global dependencies, while convolutional neural networks excel at extracting local features. Combining these approaches can enhance performance in multimodal remote sensing image classification. However, most multimodal methods fail to fully exploit the complementary semantic information between modalities, hampering the potential of multimodal data. Meanwhile, most multimodal methods often have high model complexity, limiting their application. This paper proposes the Multimodal Semantic-Scale Network (MuSeS-Net), which improves remote sensing image classification accuracy and efficiency by fully leveraging semantic features for the fusion of hyperspectral image (HSI) and Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) data. The Semantic Euclidean Attention Module (SEAM) employs a Euclidean-based semantic attention mechanism together with center-anchored reasoning to achieve expressive global modeling, effectively balancing contextual integration with local semantic fidelity across modalities. The Hierarchical Pooling and Refinement Module (HPRM) is designed as an efficient scale-adaptive module that balances dimensionality reduction and feature recovery, thereby enhancing semantic alignment and representation while capturing both local details and global dependencies with reduced computational overhead. Finally, MuSeS-Net completes the classification by using a simple fully connected layer, resulting in a lightweight multimodal model. Experimental results on three multimodal remote sensing datasets show that MuSeS-Net achieves superior classification performance with much fewer parameters and less training time, where MuSeS-Net achieves an improvement of up to 1.46 percentage points in overall accuracy, reduces training time by approximately 27.15% and cuts parameter count by approximately 61.49%, compared to the best baseline method. Our publicly available code is at https://github.com/mabokun618/MuSeS-Net-master-main . Published: 2026-04-07T15:27:30+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.843 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Caihong Mu; Bokun Ma; Yi Liu

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104361

Relevance: 0.843 (must_read)

Self-attention mechanisms specialize in capturing global dependencies, while convolutional neural networks excel at extracting local features. Combining these approaches can enhance performance in multimodal remote sensing image classification. However, most multimodal methods fail to fully exploit the complementary semantic information between modalities, hampering the potential of multimodal data. Meanwhile, most multimodal methods often have high model complexity, limiting their application. This paper proposes the Multimodal Semantic-Scale Network (MuSeS-Net), which improves remote sensing image classification accuracy and efficiency by fully leveraging semantic features for the fusion of hyperspectral image (HSI) and Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) data. The Semantic Euclidean Attention Module (SEAM) employs a Euclidean-based semantic attention mechanism together with center-anchored reasoning to achieve expressive global modeling, effectively balancing contextual integration with local semantic fidelity across modalities. The Hierarchical Pooling and Refinement Module (HPRM) is designed as an efficient scale-adaptive module that balances dimensionality reduction and feature recovery, thereby enhancing semantic alignment and representation while capturing both local details and global dependencies with reduced computational overhead. Finally, MuSeS-Net completes the classification by using a simple fully connected layer, resulting in a lightweight multimodal model. Experimental results on three multimodal remote sensing datasets show that MuSeS-Net achieves superior classification performance with much fewer parameters and less training time, where MuSeS-Net achieves an improvement of up to 1.46 percentage points in overall accuracy, reduces training time by approximately 27.15% and cuts parameter count by approximately 61.49%, compared to the best baseline method. Our publicly available code is at https://github.com/mabokun618/MuSeS-Net-master-main .

item.title: IRSD-DETR: A lightweight real-time detection transformer for infrared ship detection
item.dc:creator: Pengqiang Ge
Guohua Gu
Weixian Qian
Xiaofang Kong
Qian Chen
Minjie Wan
item.prism:publicationName: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
item.description: Infrared ship detection (IRSD) plays an important role in many applications such as thermal remote sensing for maritime safety and surveillance. However, its detection performance often degrades due to small target size, insufficient feature extraction and complex background interference, leading to frequent false alarms and missed detections. In addition, existing detection transformer (DETR)-based models suffer from heavy network parameters and high computational complexity. To solve these issues, we propose a lightweight real-time detection transformer for IRSD, termed IRSD-DETR. Firstly, four lightweight wavelet-based transform blocks (WTBs) significantly decrease the network parameters and computational complexity while maintaining decent detection accuracy and enhancing the multi-scale representation of infrared (IR) ships. Secondly, within the effective hybrid encoder (EHE), the attention-based intra-scale feature interaction adaptive sparse self-attention (AIFI_ASSA) module effectively suppresses noisy interactions from irrelevant areas while retaining the most informative features of IR ships. The lightweight relative position enhanced self-attention mechanism (RPESAM) module expands the receptive field without introducing extra computational costs. The pinwheel-shaped convolution (PSConv) substitutes the standard convolution, significantly reducing the loss of fine details of IR ships. Lastly, the normalized Gaussian Wasserstein distance (NWD) further enhances the stability and accuracy of bounding box regression for IRSD. Compared with the baseline RT-DETR-r18, our IRSD-DETR model achieves a 42.7% reduction in network parameters, a 58.7% reduction in floating point operations (FLOPs), a 1.9% increment in precision, a 2.0% increment in recall, a 2.0% increment in F1 score, a 2.4% increment in mAP@0.5(%), and a 0.8% increment in mAP@0.5:0.95(%). Our codes are available at https://github.com/MinjieWan/IRSD-DETR . Published: 2026-04-06T10:27:02+00:00 Venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Score: 0.837 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Pengqiang Ge; Guohua Gu; Weixian Qian; Xiaofang Kong; Qian Chen; Minjie Wan

Journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1016/j.isprsjprs.2026.03.048

Relevance: 0.837 (must_read)

Infrared ship detection (IRSD) plays an important role in many applications such as thermal remote sensing for maritime safety and surveillance. However, its detection performance often degrades due to small target size, insufficient feature extraction and complex background interference, leading to frequent false alarms and missed detections. In addition, existing detection transformer (DETR)-based models suffer from heavy network parameters and high computational complexity. To solve these issues, we propose a lightweight real-time detection transformer for IRSD, termed IRSD-DETR. Firstly, four lightweight wavelet-based transform blocks (WTBs) significantly decrease the network parameters and computational complexity while maintaining decent detection accuracy and enhancing the multi-scale representation of infrared (IR) ships. Secondly, within the effective hybrid encoder (EHE), the attention-based intra-scale feature interaction adaptive sparse self-attention (AIFI_ASSA) module effectively suppresses noisy interactions from irrelevant areas while retaining the most informative features of IR ships. The lightweight relative position enhanced self-attention mechanism (RPESAM) module expands the receptive field without introducing extra computational costs. The pinwheel-shaped convolution (PSConv) substitutes the standard convolution, significantly reducing the loss of fine details of IR ships. Lastly, the normalized Gaussian Wasserstein distance (NWD) further enhances the stability and accuracy of bounding box regression for IRSD. Compared with the baseline RT-DETR-r18, our IRSD-DETR model achieves a 42.7% reduction in network parameters, a 58.7% reduction in floating point operations (FLOPs), a 1.9% increment in precision, a 2.0% increment in recall, a 2.0% increment in F1 score, a 2.4% increment in mAP@0.5(%), and a 0.8% increment in mAP@0.5:0.95(%). Our codes are available at https://github.com/MinjieWan/IRSD-DETR .

item.title: Feature-guided multi-stage generative adversarial network for SAR image generation
item.dc:creator: Ming Liu
Yixuan Du
Shichao Chen
Mingliang Tao
Yifei Fan
item.prism:publicationName: Expert Systems with Applications
item.description: At present, relatively few image generations focus on the characteristics of synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images. Focusing on the problem of SAR image generation, a feature-guided multi-stage generative adversarial network (FGMS-GAN) algorithm is proposed in this paper, which mainly consists of three stages: the background stage, the shape stage, and the target stage. In the background stage, the statistical characteristics of SAR images are modeled by using the G 0 distribution to ensure that the generated images conform to the statistical properties of real SAR images. In the shape stage, the azimuth angles of SAR images are used to achieve higher similarity in shape and geometry between the generated and real SAR images. In the target stage, the scattering center characteristics of SAR images are extracted and applied to the generative network of target stage, which can enhance the detailed representation of the target and improve recognition and clarity in the target region. The moving and stationary target recognition (MSTAR) dataset is utilized to train and generate SAR images. Experimental results have verified the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm. Published: 2026-04-07T15:23:59+00:00 Venue: Expert Systems with Applications Score: 0.836 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Ming Liu; Yixuan Du; Shichao Chen; Mingliang Tao; Yifei Fan

Journal: Expert Systems with Applications

DOI: 10.1016/j.eswa.2026.132173

Relevance: 0.836 (must_read)

At present, relatively few image generations focus on the characteristics of synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images. Focusing on the problem of SAR image generation, a feature-guided multi-stage generative adversarial network (FGMS-GAN) algorithm is proposed in this paper, which mainly consists of three stages: the background stage, the shape stage, and the target stage. In the background stage, the statistical characteristics of SAR images are modeled by using the G 0 distribution to ensure that the generated images conform to the statistical properties of real SAR images. In the shape stage, the azimuth angles of SAR images are used to achieve higher similarity in shape and geometry between the generated and real SAR images. In the target stage, the scattering center characteristics of SAR images are extracted and applied to the generative network of target stage, which can enhance the detailed representation of the target and improve recognition and clarity in the target region. The moving and stationary target recognition (MSTAR) dataset is utilized to train and generate SAR images. Experimental results have verified the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

item.title: DI3CL: Contrastive Learning With Dynamic Instances and Contour Consistency for SAR Land-Cover Classification Foundation Model
item.dc:creator: Zhongle Ren
Hui Ding
Kai Wang
Biao Hou
Xingyu Luo
Weibin Li
Licheng Jiao
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
item.description: Although significant advances have been achieved in SAR land-cover classification, recent methods remain predominantly focused on supervised learning, which relies heavily on extensive labeled datasets. This dependency not only limits scalability and generalization but also restricts adaptability to diverse application scenarios. In this paper, a general-purpose foundation model for SAR land-cover classification is developed, serving as a robust cornerstone to accelerate the development and deployment of various downstream models. Specifically, a Dynamic Instance and Contour Consistency Contrastive Learning (DI3CL) pre-training framework is presented, which incorporates a Dynamic Instance (DI) module and a Contour Consistency (CC) module. DI module enhances global contextual awareness by enforcing local consistency across different views of the same region. CC module leverages shallow feature maps to guide the model to focus on the geometric contours of SAR land-cover objects, thereby improving structural discrimination. Additionally, to enhance robustness and generalization during pre-training, a large-scale and diverse dataset named SARSense, comprising 460,532 SAR images, is constructed to enable the model to capture comprehensive and representative features. To evaluate the generalization capability of our foundation model, we conducted extensive experiments across a variety of SAR land-cover classification tasks, including SAR land-cover mapping, water detection, and road extraction. The results consistently demonstrate that the proposed DI3CL outperforms existing methods. Our code and pre-trained weights are publicly available at: https://github.com/SARpre-train/DI3CL. Published: 2026-04-06T19:59:19+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.834 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Zhongle Ren; Hui Ding; Kai Wang; Biao Hou; Xingyu Luo; Weibin Li; Licheng Jiao

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3678005

Relevance: 0.834 (must_read)

Although significant advances have been achieved in SAR land-cover classification, recent methods remain predominantly focused on supervised learning, which relies heavily on extensive labeled datasets. This dependency not only limits scalability and generalization but also restricts adaptability to diverse application scenarios. In this paper, a general-purpose foundation model for SAR land-cover classification is developed, serving as a robust cornerstone to accelerate the development and deployment of various downstream models. Specifically, a Dynamic Instance and Contour Consistency Contrastive Learning (DI3CL) pre-training framework is presented, which incorporates a Dynamic Instance (DI) module and a Contour Consistency (CC) module. DI module enhances global contextual awareness by enforcing local consistency across different views of the same region. CC module leverages shallow feature maps to guide the model to focus on the geometric contours of SAR land-cover objects, thereby improving structural discrimination. Additionally, to enhance robustness and generalization during pre-training, a large-scale and diverse dataset named SARSense, comprising 460,532 SAR images, is constructed to enable the model to capture comprehensive and representative features. To evaluate the generalization capability of our foundation model, we conducted extensive experiments across a variety of SAR land-cover classification tasks, including SAR land-cover mapping, water detection, and road extraction. The results consistently demonstrate that the proposed DI3CL outperforms existing methods. Our code and pre-trained weights are publicly available at: https://github.com/SARpre-train/DI3CL.

item.title: Deep Information-Balanced Multimodal Learning
item.dc:creator: Yang Qin
Yanglin Feng
Yuan Sun
Dezhong Peng
Xi Peng
Peng Hu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Multimodal learning aims to integrate diverse data sources to capture more comprehensive information about things, thus enhancing perception and understanding of the real world. However, inherent discrepancies between different modalities often lead to imbalanced optimization during multimodal learning, hindering performance improvement. To address this issue, in this paper, we present a Multimodal Information Balance (MIB) theory, grounded in Information Theory, to reveal that this imbalance arises from the imbalanced retention of complementary information during modality fusion, providing an intuitive and explainable perspective on the issue. Building on this insight, we propose a theoretical MIB criterion to adaptively balance the preservation of complementary information across individual modalities, thereby facilitating multimodal fusion. Using this criterion, we develop an Information-Balanced Multimodal Learning (IBML) framework to mine comprehensive and balanced multimodal information, achieving optimal learning. More specifically, IBML introduces Balance Information Optimization (BIO) module to maximize tractable lower bound objectives derived from the MIB criterion according to the optimization discrepancies across modalities, ensuring balanced retention of complementary information and enhancing information contributions during multimodal fusion. In addition, we present a supplementary and provable Task Complexity Modulation (TCM) module based on the MIB criterion to adjust task complexity discrepancies across input modalities, thus indirectly promoting the balanced preservation of complementary information throughout the learning process. Extensive experiments are conducted on eight multimodal datasets, spanning audio-visual recognition, image-text classification, and 2D-3D recognition, to verify the superiority and effectiveness of IBML. The code will be released publicly after in-peer review. Published: 2026-04-07T19:59:33+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.834 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yang Qin; Yanglin Feng; Yuan Sun; Dezhong Peng; Xi Peng; Peng Hu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3681770

Relevance: 0.834 (must_read)

Multimodal learning aims to integrate diverse data sources to capture more comprehensive information about things, thus enhancing perception and understanding of the real world. However, inherent discrepancies between different modalities often lead to imbalanced optimization during multimodal learning, hindering performance improvement. To address this issue, in this paper, we present a Multimodal Information Balance (MIB) theory, grounded in Information Theory, to reveal that this imbalance arises from the imbalanced retention of complementary information during modality fusion, providing an intuitive and explainable perspective on the issue. Building on this insight, we propose a theoretical MIB criterion to adaptively balance the preservation of complementary information across individual modalities, thereby facilitating multimodal fusion. Using this criterion, we develop an Information-Balanced Multimodal Learning (IBML) framework to mine comprehensive and balanced multimodal information, achieving optimal learning. More specifically, IBML introduces Balance Information Optimization (BIO) module to maximize tractable lower bound objectives derived from the MIB criterion according to the optimization discrepancies across modalities, ensuring balanced retention of complementary information and enhancing information contributions during multimodal fusion. In addition, we present a supplementary and provable Task Complexity Modulation (TCM) module based on the MIB criterion to adjust task complexity discrepancies across input modalities, thus indirectly promoting the balanced preservation of complementary information throughout the learning process. Extensive experiments are conducted on eight multimodal datasets, spanning audio-visual recognition, image-text classification, and 2D-3D recognition, to verify the superiority and effectiveness of IBML. The code will be released publicly after in-peer review.

item.title: SAMamba: Stream Alignment Mamba for Motion Infrared Small Target Detection
item.dc:creator: Yi Sui
Xiyang Zhi
Yuanxin Huang
Tianjun Shi
Shikai Jiang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: The key to infrared small target detection lies in dis-tinguishing targets from backgrounds for accurate extraction. However, long-distance imaging with low angular resolution causes morphology loss and weak saliency, making targets easily submerged in complex backgrounds. As a result, achieving high-probability, low-false-alarm detection solely in the spatial domain is challenging. To address this, we extend to the spatiotemporal domain and propose Stream Alignment Mamba for Motion In-frared Small Target Detection (SAMamba). Specifically, we first design a Stream Mamba Block based on a dynamic state space model, which introduces bidirectional spatiotemporal scanning to explore inter-frame relationships in sequential images, not only preserving spatial correlations but also leveraging the causality of temporal cues for comprehensive global background representa-tion. Second, we develop a Cross-Frame Feature Enhancement Block to implicitly align feature streams by computing local re-sponses between neighboring and reference frames, thereby strengthening weak target features. In addition, we devise a Hi-erarchical Feature Alignment Block with adaptive adjustment strategies and position-aware mechanisms to address spatial mis-alignment during upsampling, thus enhancing the effectiveness of multi-scale target fusion and ultimately improving detection ac-curacy. Extensive experiments on IRDST, IRSatVideo-LEO, and TSIRMT datasets demonstrate that our method achieves signifi-cant advantages over state-of-the-art (SOTA) approaches. Published: 2026-04-06T19:58:56+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.830 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yi Sui; Xiyang Zhi; Yuanxin Huang; Tianjun Shi; Shikai Jiang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3681127

Relevance: 0.830 (must_read)

The key to infrared small target detection lies in dis-tinguishing targets from backgrounds for accurate extraction. However, long-distance imaging with low angular resolution causes morphology loss and weak saliency, making targets easily submerged in complex backgrounds. As a result, achieving high-probability, low-false-alarm detection solely in the spatial domain is challenging. To address this, we extend to the spatiotemporal domain and propose Stream Alignment Mamba for Motion In-frared Small Target Detection (SAMamba). Specifically, we first design a Stream Mamba Block based on a dynamic state space model, which introduces bidirectional spatiotemporal scanning to explore inter-frame relationships in sequential images, not only preserving spatial correlations but also leveraging the causality of temporal cues for comprehensive global background representa-tion. Second, we develop a Cross-Frame Feature Enhancement Block to implicitly align feature streams by computing local re-sponses between neighboring and reference frames, thereby strengthening weak target features. In addition, we devise a Hi-erarchical Feature Alignment Block with adaptive adjustment strategies and position-aware mechanisms to address spatial mis-alignment during upsampling, thus enhancing the effectiveness of multi-scale target fusion and ultimately improving detection ac-curacy. Extensive experiments on IRDST, IRSatVideo-LEO, and TSIRMT datasets demonstrate that our method achieves signifi-cant advantages over state-of-the-art (SOTA) approaches.

item.title: High-quality cloud-free optical image generation using multi-temporal SAR and contaminated optical data
item.dc:creator: Chenxi Duan
Mariana Belgiu
Alfred Stein
item.prism:publicationName: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
item.description: This paper addresses the challenge of missing optical image generation, i.e., generating unavailable optical images, for instance, due to cloud cover. We propose CRGenNet, an image generation network that incorporates innovative modules, for instance the DownUp Block and Fusion Attention to enhance accuracy. Furthermore, we constructed the TCSEN12 dataset, which includes multi-temporal SAR and optical observations in a flood-affected region, offering a benchmark for evaluating image generation methods under challenging conditions, characterized by cloud-contaminated auxiliary imagery and substantial temporal change. Experimental results validate the effectiveness, achieving superior visual effects and quantitative evaluation results that exceed those produced by comparison methods. On the TCSEN12 dataset, it achieved a Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) of 26.978 and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) of 0.648, as compared to 26.225 and 0.622, using MTS2ONet, while on the public DTSEN1-2 dataset, it achieved a PSNR of 88.9263 and SSIM of 0.9999, as compared to 36.0435 PSNR and 0.9896 SSIM, respectively, for MTS2ONet. The study concludes that CRGenNet provides a robust method for integrating multi-modal data with complex temporal dynamics, while TCSEN12 establishes a more realistic benchmark by focusing on future-scene prediction under challenging cloud and post-disaster conditions. Published: 2026-04-06T10:27:02+00:00 Venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Score: 0.818 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Chenxi Duan; Mariana Belgiu; Alfred Stein

Journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1016/j.isprsjprs.2026.03.042

Relevance: 0.818 (must_read)

This paper addresses the challenge of missing optical image generation, i.e., generating unavailable optical images, for instance, due to cloud cover. We propose CRGenNet, an image generation network that incorporates innovative modules, for instance the DownUp Block and Fusion Attention to enhance accuracy. Furthermore, we constructed the TCSEN12 dataset, which includes multi-temporal SAR and optical observations in a flood-affected region, offering a benchmark for evaluating image generation methods under challenging conditions, characterized by cloud-contaminated auxiliary imagery and substantial temporal change. Experimental results validate the effectiveness, achieving superior visual effects and quantitative evaluation results that exceed those produced by comparison methods. On the TCSEN12 dataset, it achieved a Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) of 26.978 and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) of 0.648, as compared to 26.225 and 0.622, using MTS2ONet, while on the public DTSEN1-2 dataset, it achieved a PSNR of 88.9263 and SSIM of 0.9999, as compared to 36.0435 PSNR and 0.9896 SSIM, respectively, for MTS2ONet. The study concludes that CRGenNet provides a robust method for integrating multi-modal data with complex temporal dynamics, while TCSEN12 establishes a more realistic benchmark by focusing on future-scene prediction under challenging cloud and post-disaster conditions.

item.title: Scattering-Aware Graph Neural Network with Transfer Learning for Few-shot Aircraft Detection in SAR Images
item.dc:creator: Qian Guo
Jiyun Chen
Jingjing Zhang
Hui Bi
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
item.description: Deep neural networks integrating explainable information have shown significant progress in synthetic aperture radar-automatic target recognition (SAR-ATR). Nevertheless, their underlying mechanisms and performance in few-shot object detection (FSOD) scenarios remain insufficiently explored. From a disentanglement perspective, FSOD can be decomposed into a detection task followed by a classification task. The former emphasizes discriminative features between targets and background for localization, while the latter focuses on intra-class distinctive attributes for fine-grained classification. Therefore, this paper proposes a scattering-aware graph neural network (SAGN) with transfer learning for aircraft detection in SAR imagery. The framework explicitly leverages scattering-aware prior knowledge tailored for the two subtasks, comprising two core components: a salient feature generator (SFG) for localization and a structure feature injector (SFI) for classification. First, we analyze the effectiveness of applying disentangled prior knowledge in FSOD tasks. Subsequently, the SFG module is introduced for localization, consisting of a salient feature extraction module (SEM) that enhances distinctive target-background features, and a context-aware module (CAM) that captures global spatial dependencies through expanded receptive fields. For fine-grained classification, the SFI module incorporates structural and semantic target features to enable efficient representation learning under limited samples. Especially, a triplet loss based on anchor, positive, and negative topological features is designed to improve generalization in a self-supervised manner. Furthermore, a transfer learning strategy leveraging large-scale base-class data is devised to enhance few-shot adaptation. Comprehensive experiments on the SAR-AIRcraft-1.0 dataset demonstrate that SAGN exhibits outstanding performance, with 0.507 {mAP}\_ ext{novel} {mAP}\_ ext{novel} , 0.808 {mAP}\_ ext{base} {mAP}\_ ext{base} , and 0.722 ${mAP}\_ ext... Published: 2026-04-07T20:00:09+00:00 Venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing Score: 0.816 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Qian Guo; Jiyun Chen; Jingjing Zhang; Hui Bi

Journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/jstars.2026.3680649

Relevance: 0.816 (must_read)

Deep neural networks integrating explainable information have shown significant progress in synthetic aperture radar-automatic target recognition (SAR-ATR). Nevertheless, their underlying mechanisms and performance in few-shot object detection (FSOD) scenarios remain insufficiently explored. From a disentanglement perspective, FSOD can be decomposed into a detection task followed by a classification task. The former emphasizes discriminative features between targets and background for localization, while the latter focuses on intra-class distinctive attributes for fine-grained classification. Therefore, this paper proposes a scattering-aware graph neural network (SAGN) with transfer learning for aircraft detection in SAR imagery. The framework explicitly leverages scattering-aware prior knowledge tailored for the two subtasks, comprising two core components: a salient feature generator (SFG) for localization and a structure feature injector (SFI) for classification. First, we analyze the effectiveness of applying disentangled prior knowledge in FSOD tasks. Subsequently, the SFG module is introduced for localization, consisting of a salient feature extraction module (SEM) that enhances distinctive target-background features, and a context-aware module (CAM) that captures global spatial dependencies through expanded receptive fields. For fine-grained classification, the SFI module incorporates structural and semantic target features to enable efficient representation learning under limited samples. Especially, a triplet loss based on anchor, positive, and negative topological features is designed to improve generalization in a self-supervised manner. Furthermore, a transfer learning strategy leveraging large-scale base-class data is devised to enhance few-shot adaptation. Comprehensive experiments on the SAR-AIRcraft-1.0 dataset demonstrate that SAGN exhibits outstanding performance, with 0.507 {mAP}\_ ext{novel} {mAP}\_ ext{novel} , 0.808 {mAP}\_ ext{base} {mAP}\_ ext{base} , and 0.722 ${mAP}\_ ext...

item.title: AdaNoise: Cycle-Consistent Image Translation with Domain-Adaptive Noise Perturbation
item.dc:creator: Xi Yang
Haoyuan Shi
Fei Gao
Nannan Wang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: Image-to-image (I2I) translation aims to transform an input image into a target domain while preserving its structural details. Recent advances in diffusion-based generative models have significantly improved the perceptual quality of generated images; however, these approaches still face challenges in controllability and consistency, largely due to the inherent randomness introduced by stochastic noise during the generation process. Specifically, directly manipulating noise distributions without semantic alignment can lead to mode collapse, texture distortion, or loss of domain-specific features. To overcome these challenges, we propose the Adaptive Noise Framework (AdaNoise), a novel and cycle-consistent I2I translation approach guided by domain-adaptive noise modulation. AdaNoise introduces a Domain-Adaptive Noise Perturbation (DANP) module, which adaptively learns structured noise patterns aligned with the target domain distribution, enhancing both the expressiveness and reliability of the translation process. Through integration with a Cycle-Consistent Dual Diffusion (CDD) architecture, AdaNoise ensures faithful content reconstruction while allowing semantically meaningful domain shifts. The framework is designed to maintain a balance between generation quality and controllability, enabling more faithful and flexible image translation. Extensive experiments on two tasks, including SAR-to-optical image translation and low-light image enhancement, validate that AdaNoise not only surpasses existing state-of-the-art methods in terms of image fidelity and semantic preservation, but also achieves more controllable and diverse outputs across varying conditions, thus offering a robust and scalable solution for cross-domain visual generation. Published: 2026-04-06T19:58:56+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.815 (must_read)

Authors: Xi Yang; Haoyuan Shi; Fei Gao; Nannan Wang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3681274

Relevance: 0.815 (must_read)

Image-to-image (I2I) translation aims to transform an input image into a target domain while preserving its structural details. Recent advances in diffusion-based generative models have significantly improved the perceptual quality of generated images; however, these approaches still face challenges in controllability and consistency, largely due to the inherent randomness introduced by stochastic noise during the generation process. Specifically, directly manipulating noise distributions without semantic alignment can lead to mode collapse, texture distortion, or loss of domain-specific features. To overcome these challenges, we propose the Adaptive Noise Framework (AdaNoise), a novel and cycle-consistent I2I translation approach guided by domain-adaptive noise modulation. AdaNoise introduces a Domain-Adaptive Noise Perturbation (DANP) module, which adaptively learns structured noise patterns aligned with the target domain distribution, enhancing both the expressiveness and reliability of the translation process. Through integration with a Cycle-Consistent Dual Diffusion (CDD) architecture, AdaNoise ensures faithful content reconstruction while allowing semantically meaningful domain shifts. The framework is designed to maintain a balance between generation quality and controllability, enabling more faithful and flexible image translation. Extensive experiments on two tasks, including SAR-to-optical image translation and low-light image enhancement, validate that AdaNoise not only surpasses existing state-of-the-art methods in terms of image fidelity and semantic preservation, but also achieves more controllable and diverse outputs across varying conditions, thus offering a robust and scalable solution for cross-domain visual generation.

item.title: SkyFind: A Large-Scale Benchmark Unveiling Referring Expression Comprehension for UAV
item.dc:creator: Kunyu Wang
Guanbo Wu
Xueyang Fu
Xingbo Wang
Kean Liu
Xin Lu
Chengjie Ge
Wei Zhai
Zheng-Jun Zha
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAV) are increasingly deployed to assist humans in diverse tasks, where understanding human intentions is critical to effective collaboration. Referring expression comprehension (REC) links language to visual targets, allowing UAV to recognize human-intended targets of interest, thereby supporting subsequent actions. However, existing REC research is almost exclusively confined to ground-based scenarios, leaving aerial scenarios largely unexplored. In this paper, we formally define UAV-based REC as a new research problem and highlight its unique challenges, including abundant background interference, small target size, and complex referring relations. To enable systematic study, we introduce SkyFind, a large-scale dataset with one million high-quality target-expression pairs, providing a solid foundation. In addition, we propose AerialREC, a baseline framework that reduces background interference in UAV imagery by searching for a potential target region before localization. We establish benchmark results on SkyFind using ten representative REC methods and validate the effectiveness of the AerialREC framework. Published: 2026-04-06T19:56:01+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.813 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Kunyu Wang; Guanbo Wu; Xueyang Fu; Xingbo Wang; Kean Liu; Xin Lu; Chengjie Ge; Wei Zhai; Zheng-Jun Zha

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3681112

Relevance: 0.813 (must_read)

Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAV) are increasingly deployed to assist humans in diverse tasks, where understanding human intentions is critical to effective collaboration. Referring expression comprehension (REC) links language to visual targets, allowing UAV to recognize human-intended targets of interest, thereby supporting subsequent actions. However, existing REC research is almost exclusively confined to ground-based scenarios, leaving aerial scenarios largely unexplored. In this paper, we formally define UAV-based REC as a new research problem and highlight its unique challenges, including abundant background interference, small target size, and complex referring relations. To enable systematic study, we introduce SkyFind, a large-scale dataset with one million high-quality target-expression pairs, providing a solid foundation. In addition, we propose AerialREC, a baseline framework that reduces background interference in UAV imagery by searching for a potential target region before localization. We establish benchmark results on SkyFind using ten representative REC methods and validate the effectiveness of the AerialREC framework.

item.title: GSDehazeFusion: Efficient Image Dehazing via Fusion of Multi-Scale Gaussian Pyramid and Fourier-Aware Diffusion
item.dc:creator: Bangjun Lei
Tongfa Wang
Ziyi Li
Jiaojiao Yu
Yilei Chen
Qiankun Zuo
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: Image dehazing with diffusion models often faces high inference latency and memory cost on high-resolution inputs because denoising requires many sequential reverse steps and each step evaluates a large noise-prediction network on full-resolution feature maps. To improve scalability, we propose GSDehazeFusion, a supervised dehazing framework built on IR-SDE. To reduce the inference latency and GPU memory cost of diffusion-based dehazing on high-resolution inputs, we introduce a multi-scale Gaussian pyramid module, which decomposes the input into coarse-to-fine representations so that global structures can be modeled at lower resolutions with reduced per-step spatial complexity. To improve noise prediction and enhance structural and detail recovery during the reverse diffusion process, we design a Fourier Noise-Aware Fusion Network, named FNAFNet, which performs frequency-domain feature interaction via the Fourier transform and recombines complementary spectral components to better couple spatial alignment and frequency-energy distribution in learned feature maps. To avoid over-smoothed reconstructions under purely pixel-wise supervision, we further adopt a structure-aware objective that combines L1 and MS-SSIM, balancing pixel fidelity and structural consistency. Overall, GSDehazeFusion builds on IR-SDE and integrates Gaussian pyramid multi-scale processing, Fourier-aware noise prediction, and structure-aware optimization into an end-to-end dehazing framework for high-resolution images. Experimental results demonstrate that the GSDehazeFusion method can effectively remove haze from images while preserving rich details and colors. Compared to traditional methods, it shows significant advantages. Published: 2026-04-06T06:16:23+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.810 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Bangjun Lei; Tongfa Wang; Ziyi Li; Jiaojiao Yu; Yilei Chen; Qiankun Zuo

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104340

Relevance: 0.810 (must_read)

Image dehazing with diffusion models often faces high inference latency and memory cost on high-resolution inputs because denoising requires many sequential reverse steps and each step evaluates a large noise-prediction network on full-resolution feature maps. To improve scalability, we propose GSDehazeFusion, a supervised dehazing framework built on IR-SDE. To reduce the inference latency and GPU memory cost of diffusion-based dehazing on high-resolution inputs, we introduce a multi-scale Gaussian pyramid module, which decomposes the input into coarse-to-fine representations so that global structures can be modeled at lower resolutions with reduced per-step spatial complexity. To improve noise prediction and enhance structural and detail recovery during the reverse diffusion process, we design a Fourier Noise-Aware Fusion Network, named FNAFNet, which performs frequency-domain feature interaction via the Fourier transform and recombines complementary spectral components to better couple spatial alignment and frequency-energy distribution in learned feature maps. To avoid over-smoothed reconstructions under purely pixel-wise supervision, we further adopt a structure-aware objective that combines L1 and MS-SSIM, balancing pixel fidelity and structural consistency. Overall, GSDehazeFusion builds on IR-SDE and integrates Gaussian pyramid multi-scale processing, Fourier-aware noise prediction, and structure-aware optimization into an end-to-end dehazing framework for high-resolution images. Experimental results demonstrate that the GSDehazeFusion method can effectively remove haze from images while preserving rich details and colors. Compared to traditional methods, it shows significant advantages.

item.title: A Pose-only Geometric Constraint for Multi-Camera Pose Adjustment
item.dc:creator: Shunkun Liang
Banglei Guan
Bin Li
Qifeng Yu
Yang Shang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Multi-camera systems offer rich observation capabilities for visual navigation and 3D scene reconstruction; however, the resulting feature redundancy often compromises computational efficiency. This challenge is particularly pronounced during bundle adjustment, where the non-linear optimization of both system poses and scene points incurs substantial computational overhead. To address this challenge, this paper introduces a pose-only geometric constraint for multi-camera systems and proposes a corresponding pose adjustment algorithm. Specifically, we use generalized camera model to establish a unified representation of the multi-camera system. Building upon this model, we formulate the multi-camera pose-only constraint, which implicitly represents a 3D scene point using two base observations and their associated poses, thereby achieving a pose-only representation of the projection geometry. Subsequently, we introduce a multi-camera pose adjustment algorithm that eliminates 3D points from the parameter space, thereby achieving efficient and focused pose optimization. Experimental results on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that the proposed algorithm outperforms baseline bundle adjustment methods in computational efficiency, while maintaining or even improving pose estimation accuracy Published: 2026-04-06T19:56:01+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.810 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Shunkun Liang; Banglei Guan; Bin Li; Qifeng Yu; Yang Shang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3681272

Relevance: 0.810 (must_read)

Multi-camera systems offer rich observation capabilities for visual navigation and 3D scene reconstruction; however, the resulting feature redundancy often compromises computational efficiency. This challenge is particularly pronounced during bundle adjustment, where the non-linear optimization of both system poses and scene points incurs substantial computational overhead. To address this challenge, this paper introduces a pose-only geometric constraint for multi-camera systems and proposes a corresponding pose adjustment algorithm. Specifically, we use generalized camera model to establish a unified representation of the multi-camera system. Building upon this model, we formulate the multi-camera pose-only constraint, which implicitly represents a 3D scene point using two base observations and their associated poses, thereby achieving a pose-only representation of the projection geometry. Subsequently, we introduce a multi-camera pose adjustment algorithm that eliminates 3D points from the parameter space, thereby achieving efficient and focused pose optimization. Experimental results on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that the proposed algorithm outperforms baseline bundle adjustment methods in computational efficiency, while maintaining or even improving pose estimation accuracy

item.title: iSeg: An Iterative Refinement-based Framework for Training-free Segmentation
item.dc:creator: Lin Sun
Jiale Cao
Jin Xie
Fahad Shahbaz Khan
Yanwei Pang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Stable Diffusion has demonstrated strong image synthesis ability to given text descriptions, suggesting it to contain strong semantic clue for grouping objects. The researchers have explored employing Stable Diffusion for training-free segmentation. Most existing approaches refine cross-attention map by self-attention map once, demonstrating that self-attention map contains useful semantic information to improve segmentation. To fully utilize self-attention map, we present a deep experimental analysis on iteratively refining cross-attention map with self-attention map, and propose an effective iterative refinement framework for training-free segmentation, named iSeg. Our iSeg introduces an entropy-reduced self-attention module that utilizes a gradient descent scheme to reduce the entropy of self-attention map, thereby suppressing the weak responses corresponding to irrelevant global information. Leveraging the entropy-reduced self-attention module, our iSeg stably improves cross-attention map with iterative refinement. Further, we design a category-enhanced cross-attention module to generate accurate cross-attention map, providing a better initial input for iterative refinement. Extensive experiments across different datasets and diverse segmentation tasks (weakly-supervised semantic segmentation, open-vocabulary semantic segmentation, unsupervised segmentation, and mask generation on synthetic dataset) reveal the merits of proposed contributions, leading to promising performance. For unsupervised semantic segmentation on Cityscapes, our iSeg achieves an absolute gain of 3.8 % 3.8\% in terms of mIoU compared to the best existing training-free approach in literature. Moreover, our proposed iSeg can support segmentation with different kinds of images and interactions, and also be used as a post-processing, or in different frameworks, to improve training-free segmentation. The project is available at https://linsun449.github.io/iSeg. Published: 2026-04-06T19:56:01+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.809 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Lin Sun; Jiale Cao; Jin Xie; Fahad Shahbaz Khan; Yanwei Pang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3681368

Relevance: 0.809 (must_read)

Stable Diffusion has demonstrated strong image synthesis ability to given text descriptions, suggesting it to contain strong semantic clue for grouping objects. The researchers have explored employing Stable Diffusion for training-free segmentation. Most existing approaches refine cross-attention map by self-attention map once, demonstrating that self-attention map contains useful semantic information to improve segmentation. To fully utilize self-attention map, we present a deep experimental analysis on iteratively refining cross-attention map with self-attention map, and propose an effective iterative refinement framework for training-free segmentation, named iSeg. Our iSeg introduces an entropy-reduced self-attention module that utilizes a gradient descent scheme to reduce the entropy of self-attention map, thereby suppressing the weak responses corresponding to irrelevant global information. Leveraging the entropy-reduced self-attention module, our iSeg stably improves cross-attention map with iterative refinement. Further, we design a category-enhanced cross-attention module to generate accurate cross-attention map, providing a better initial input for iterative refinement. Extensive experiments across different datasets and diverse segmentation tasks (weakly-supervised semantic segmentation, open-vocabulary semantic segmentation, unsupervised segmentation, and mask generation on synthetic dataset) reveal the merits of proposed contributions, leading to promising performance. For unsupervised semantic segmentation on Cityscapes, our iSeg achieves an absolute gain of 3.8 % 3.8\% in terms of mIoU compared to the best existing training-free approach in literature. Moreover, our proposed iSeg can support segmentation with different kinds of images and interactions, and also be used as a post-processing, or in different frameworks, to improve training-free segmentation. The project is available at https://linsun449.github.io/iSeg.

item.title: DreamFuse: Towards Realistic and Seamless Image Fusion Across Diverse Scenarios
item.dc:creator: Junjia Huang
Pengxiang Yan
Jiyang Liu
Jie Wu
Zhao Wang
Yitong Wang
Xinglong Wu
Liang Lin
Guanbin Li
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Image fusion seeks to seamlessly integrate fore ground objects with background scenes, producing realistic and harmonious fused images. While existing methods often insert objects directly, adaptive and interactive fusion—requiring contextual adaptation and foreground-background interplay—remains a challenging yet critical task. To address this, we first propose a pipeline for generating high-quality fusion data. By combining iterative in-context learning with existing tools, we curate a diverse cross-scene dataset supporting three core tasks: object integration, replacement, and attribute-referenced editing. Lever aging this, we introduce DreamFuse, a unified diffusion-based approach that jointly optimizes these capabilities. DreamFuse exploits the Diffusion Transformer (DiT) architecture, using its attention mechanism to extract and align foreground-background features for coherent fusion. For flexible control, we incorporate a Positional Affine mechanism, enabling precise spatial and scale adjustments while supporting diverse text-driven fusion. Fur thermore, we employ Localized Direct Preference Optimization (L-DPO), refining the model via human feedback to enhance harmony and consistency. Extensive experimental results demon strate DreamFuse's superiority over state-of-the-art approaches across multiple metrics. Published: 2026-04-07T19:59:33+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.809 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Junjia Huang; Pengxiang Yan; Jiyang Liu; Jie Wu; Zhao Wang; Yitong Wang; Xinglong Wu; Liang Lin; Guanbin Li

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3680535

Relevance: 0.809 (must_read)

Image fusion seeks to seamlessly integrate fore ground objects with background scenes, producing realistic and harmonious fused images. While existing methods often insert objects directly, adaptive and interactive fusion—requiring contextual adaptation and foreground-background interplay—remains a challenging yet critical task. To address this, we first propose a pipeline for generating high-quality fusion data. By combining iterative in-context learning with existing tools, we curate a diverse cross-scene dataset supporting three core tasks: object integration, replacement, and attribute-referenced editing. Lever aging this, we introduce DreamFuse, a unified diffusion-based approach that jointly optimizes these capabilities. DreamFuse exploits the Diffusion Transformer (DiT) architecture, using its attention mechanism to extract and align foreground-background features for coherent fusion. For flexible control, we incorporate a Positional Affine mechanism, enabling precise spatial and scale adjustments while supporting diverse text-driven fusion. Fur thermore, we employ Localized Direct Preference Optimization (L-DPO), refining the model via human feedback to enhance harmony and consistency. Extensive experimental results demon strate DreamFuse's superiority over state-of-the-art approaches across multiple metrics.

item.title: A lightweight multi-domain collaborative representation network for infrared small target detection
item.dc:creator: Boyuan Li
Zitong Ren
Tuersunjiang Baidi
Saisai Ma
Kurban Ubul
item.prism:publicationName: Expert Systems with Applications
item.description: The core challenge of infrared small target detection (IRSTD) is that targets are easily confused with high-brightness background clutter due to their small physical size and lack of stable features. Although many advanced models are dedicated to improving detection accuracy, their high computational costs severely hinder their application on edge devices that require real-time responses. To simultaneously address the dual challenges of accuracy and efficiency, we propose a lightweight multi-domain collaborative representation network (LW-MCRNet). First, we propose a multi-domain feature extraction module (MFEM). At a limited computational cost, it constructs a robust initial feature representation by fusing spatial, frequency, and multi-scale contextual information in parallel to effectively resist interference from background noise and artifacts effectively. Building on this, we propose a graph reasoning positioning module (GRPM), which projects features into a graph space for explicit relational reasoning, thereby precisely capturing the non-local dependencies crucial for target localization. Finally, in the decoding stage, to achieve adaptive fusion of high- and low-level features and prevent the attenuation of weak target signals, we design a dynamic coordinate attention module (DCAM). It intelligently guides the coarse-to-fine feature recovery process through a position-sensitive attention mechanism. Experimental results on multiple public datasets show that our proposed LW-MCRNet achieves detection performance comparable to or even superior to current state-of-the-art methods at an extremely low computational cost of only 0.30M parameters and 2.41G FLOPs. Published: 2026-04-06T06:14:31+00:00 Venue: Expert Systems with Applications Score: 0.809 (must_read)

Authors: Boyuan Li; Zitong Ren; Tuersunjiang Baidi; Saisai Ma; Kurban Ubul

Journal: Expert Systems with Applications

DOI: 10.1016/j.eswa.2026.132334

Relevance: 0.809 (must_read)

The core challenge of infrared small target detection (IRSTD) is that targets are easily confused with high-brightness background clutter due to their small physical size and lack of stable features. Although many advanced models are dedicated to improving detection accuracy, their high computational costs severely hinder their application on edge devices that require real-time responses. To simultaneously address the dual challenges of accuracy and efficiency, we propose a lightweight multi-domain collaborative representation network (LW-MCRNet). First, we propose a multi-domain feature extraction module (MFEM). At a limited computational cost, it constructs a robust initial feature representation by fusing spatial, frequency, and multi-scale contextual information in parallel to effectively resist interference from background noise and artifacts effectively. Building on this, we propose a graph reasoning positioning module (GRPM), which projects features into a graph space for explicit relational reasoning, thereby precisely capturing the non-local dependencies crucial for target localization. Finally, in the decoding stage, to achieve adaptive fusion of high- and low-level features and prevent the attenuation of weak target signals, we design a dynamic coordinate attention module (DCAM). It intelligently guides the coarse-to-fine feature recovery process through a position-sensitive attention mechanism. Experimental results on multiple public datasets show that our proposed LW-MCRNet achieves detection performance comparable to or even superior to current state-of-the-art methods at an extremely low computational cost of only 0.30M parameters and 2.41G FLOPs.

item.title: DepthMaster: Taming Diffusion Models for Monocular Depth Estimation
item.dc:creator: Ziyang Song
Zerong Wang
Bo Li
Hao Zhang
Ruijie Zhu
Li Liu
Peng-Tao Jiang
Tianzhu Zhang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: Monocular depth estimation within the denoising diffusion paradigm demonstrates impressive generalization ability but suffers from low inference speed. Recent methods adopt a single-step deterministic paradigm to improve inference efficiency. However, uncritically applying the generative features from diffusion models to the perceptual depth estimation task leads to suboptimal results, due to the differences between image generation and dense prediction. In this work, we propose Depth-Master, a single-step diffusion model designed to tame diffusion models for enhanced performance and visual quality. First, to mitigate overfitting to texture details introduced by generative features, we propose a Feature Alignment module, which incorporates high-quality semantic features to enhance the denoising network’s representation capability. Second, to address the lack of fine-grained details in the single-step deterministic framework, we propose a Fourier Enhancement module to adaptively balance low-frequency structure and high-frequency details. We adopt a two-stage training strategy to fully leverage the potential of the two modules. In the first stage, we focus on learning the global scene structure with the Feature Alignment module, while in the second stage, we exploit the Fourier Enhancement module to improve the visual quality. Through these efforts, our model achieves state-of-the-art performance in terms of generalization and detail preservation, outperforming other diffusion-based methods across various datasets. Our project page can be found at https://indu1ge.github.io/DepthMaster page. Published: 2026-04-06T19:58:56+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.808 (must_read)

Authors: Ziyang Song; Zerong Wang; Bo Li; Hao Zhang; Ruijie Zhu; Li Liu; Peng-Tao Jiang; Tianzhu Zhang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3681436

Relevance: 0.808 (must_read)

Monocular depth estimation within the denoising diffusion paradigm demonstrates impressive generalization ability but suffers from low inference speed. Recent methods adopt a single-step deterministic paradigm to improve inference efficiency. However, uncritically applying the generative features from diffusion models to the perceptual depth estimation task leads to suboptimal results, due to the differences between image generation and dense prediction. In this work, we propose Depth-Master, a single-step diffusion model designed to tame diffusion models for enhanced performance and visual quality. First, to mitigate overfitting to texture details introduced by generative features, we propose a Feature Alignment module, which incorporates high-quality semantic features to enhance the denoising network’s representation capability. Second, to address the lack of fine-grained details in the single-step deterministic framework, we propose a Fourier Enhancement module to adaptively balance low-frequency structure and high-frequency details. We adopt a two-stage training strategy to fully leverage the potential of the two modules. In the first stage, we focus on learning the global scene structure with the Feature Alignment module, while in the second stage, we exploit the Fourier Enhancement module to improve the visual quality. Through these efforts, our model achieves state-of-the-art performance in terms of generalization and detail preservation, outperforming other diffusion-based methods across various datasets. Our project page can be found at https://indu1ge.github.io/DepthMaster page.

item.title: Towards Memory-efficient Hyperspectral Image Reconstruction via Consistency Learning
item.dc:creator: Yihong Leng
Jiaojiao Li
Rui Song
Haitao Xu
Yunsong Li
Qian Du
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
item.description: Spectral reconstruction (SR) aims to recover high-quality hyperspectral images (HSIs) from more readily available RGB or multispectral images (MSIs). While supervised SR has shown promising results, it is hindered by the difficulty of collecting abundant, well-registered RGB-HSI or MSI-HSI pairs. Semi-supervised SR (Semi-SR) offers a more practical solution by exploiting plentiful RGBs/MSIs together with limited HSIs. However, existing Semi-SR approaches still suffer from cross-domain discrepancies, cross-modality inconsistency, and unreliable pseudo-labels. To tackle these challenges, we propose a Manifold-aware Teacher-Student Semi-SR (MTSSR) framework, which seamlessly integrates labeled and unlabeled domains through a teacher-student paradigm and memory-efficient consistency learning. At its core, a Flexible Cross-attention Spectral Reconstruction (FCSR) network extracts scene-related spatial cues via customized self-attention and models scene-agnostic priors through dynamic quantization, thereby enhancing spectral fidelity. Furthermore, a manifold-aware dimensionality analysis derives a latent space that jointly captures spatial and spectral structures across modalities. This enables a manifold-aware alignment loss to enforce cross-modality consistency and a manifold-aware contrastive loss to progressively refine pseudo-label reliability. In addition, we develop a Threshold-adjusted Memory Bank Update (TMBU) strategy, which generates reliable negative samples by storing network-driven representations instead of memory-consuming HSIs, significantly reducing memory consumption. Extensive experiments on three visual and two remote sensing benchmarks demonstrate that MTSSR consistently outperforms state-of-the-art SR methods, achieving robust and memory-efficient spectral reconstruction. Published: 2026-04-07T20:03:23+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.804 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yihong Leng; Jiaojiao Li; Rui Song; Haitao Xu; Yunsong Li; Qian Du

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3680005

Relevance: 0.804 (must_read)

Spectral reconstruction (SR) aims to recover high-quality hyperspectral images (HSIs) from more readily available RGB or multispectral images (MSIs). While supervised SR has shown promising results, it is hindered by the difficulty of collecting abundant, well-registered RGB-HSI or MSI-HSI pairs. Semi-supervised SR (Semi-SR) offers a more practical solution by exploiting plentiful RGBs/MSIs together with limited HSIs. However, existing Semi-SR approaches still suffer from cross-domain discrepancies, cross-modality inconsistency, and unreliable pseudo-labels. To tackle these challenges, we propose a Manifold-aware Teacher-Student Semi-SR (MTSSR) framework, which seamlessly integrates labeled and unlabeled domains through a teacher-student paradigm and memory-efficient consistency learning. At its core, a Flexible Cross-attention Spectral Reconstruction (FCSR) network extracts scene-related spatial cues via customized self-attention and models scene-agnostic priors through dynamic quantization, thereby enhancing spectral fidelity. Furthermore, a manifold-aware dimensionality analysis derives a latent space that jointly captures spatial and spectral structures across modalities. This enables a manifold-aware alignment loss to enforce cross-modality consistency and a manifold-aware contrastive loss to progressively refine pseudo-label reliability. In addition, we develop a Threshold-adjusted Memory Bank Update (TMBU) strategy, which generates reliable negative samples by storing network-driven representations instead of memory-consuming HSIs, significantly reducing memory consumption. Extensive experiments on three visual and two remote sensing benchmarks demonstrate that MTSSR consistently outperforms state-of-the-art SR methods, achieving robust and memory-efficient spectral reconstruction.

item.title: NoisePO: Efficient Semantic Noise Generation and Ranking for Diffusion-Based Text-to-Image Synthesis
item.dc:creator: Fuxiang Wu
Liu Liu
Fusheng Hao
Chengqun Song
Dacheng Tao
Jun Cheng
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
item.description: Diffusion-based methods have achieved remarkable success in photorealistic image generation, leveraging iterative denoising steps to improve image quality. However, multi-step denoising often suffers from error accumulation—similar to exposure bias in autoregressive models—due to suboptimal noise estimation, which can lead to degraded semantic alignment and image fidelity. To tackle the challenge of suboptimal inner latent representations in generation and improve the inner latent, this paper introduces a novel method NoisePO, an efficient semantic noise preference optimization framework. NoisePO employs a semantic noise preference optimization generative adversarial network (NPO-GAN) and noise ranking methods to search for semantically relevant noises based on textual conditions, thus eliminating undesired semantic features while emphasizing the necessary semantic ones. Specifically, NoisePO utilizes a light NPO-GAN to generate semantic noises that encourage the latent at the previous step to incorporate more semantic information from the caption. Then, light ranking models are employed to filter out low-quality noises and select the best noise. Experimental results demonstrate that NoisePO consistently outperforms the baselines across widely used frameworks, achieving notable improvements in image quality, semantic consistency, and user-specific alignment as measured by IS, FID, CLIP, and other metrics. These results indicate that NoisePO effectively enhances synthesis quality and strengthens text-image alignment. Published: 2026-04-06T19:59:19+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.798 (must_read)
