Authors: Fuxiang Wu; Liu Liu; Fusheng Hao; Chengqun Song; Dacheng Tao; Jun Cheng

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3675408

Relevance: 0.798 (must_read)

Diffusion-based methods have achieved remarkable success in photorealistic image generation, leveraging iterative denoising steps to improve image quality. However, multi-step denoising often suffers from error accumulation—similar to exposure bias in autoregressive models—due to suboptimal noise estimation, which can lead to degraded semantic alignment and image fidelity. To tackle the challenge of suboptimal inner latent representations in generation and improve the inner latent, this paper introduces a novel method NoisePO, an efficient semantic noise preference optimization framework. NoisePO employs a semantic noise preference optimization generative adversarial network (NPO-GAN) and noise ranking methods to search for semantically relevant noises based on textual conditions, thus eliminating undesired semantic features while emphasizing the necessary semantic ones. Specifically, NoisePO utilizes a light NPO-GAN to generate semantic noises that encourage the latent at the previous step to incorporate more semantic information from the caption. Then, light ranking models are employed to filter out low-quality noises and select the best noise. Experimental results demonstrate that NoisePO consistently outperforms the baselines across widely used frameworks, achieving notable improvements in image quality, semantic consistency, and user-specific alignment as measured by IS, FID, CLIP, and other metrics. These results indicate that NoisePO effectively enhances synthesis quality and strengthens text-image alignment.

item.title: Semantic Image Synthesis via Diffusion Models
item.dc:creator: Wengang Zhou
Weilun Wang
Jianmin Bao
Dongdong Chen
Dong Chen
Lu Yuan
Houqiang Li
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
item.description: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPMs) have achieved remarkable success in various image generation tasks compared with Generative Adversarial Nets (GANs). Recent work on semantic image synthesis mainly follows the de facto GAN-based approaches, which may lead to unsatisfactory quality or diversity of generated images. In this paper, we propose a novel framework based on DDPM for semantic image synthesis. Unlike previous conditional diffusion model directly feeds the semantic layout and noisy image as input to a U-Net structure, which may not fully leverage the information in the input semantic mask, our framework processes semantic layout and noisy image differently. It feeds noisy image to the encoder of the U-Net structure while the semantic layout to the decoder by multi-layer spatially-adaptive normalization operators. To further improve the generation quality and semantic interpretability in semantic image synthesis, we introduce the classifier-free guidance sampling strategy, which acknowledge the scores of an unconditional model for sampling process. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method, achieving state-of-the-art performance in terms of fidelity (FID) and diversity (LPIPS). Published: 2026-04-06T19:57:19+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia Score: 0.797 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Wengang Zhou; Weilun Wang; Jianmin Bao; Dongdong Chen; Dong Chen; Lu Yuan; Houqiang Li

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia

DOI: 10.1109/tmm.2026.3680594

Relevance: 0.797 (must_read)

Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPMs) have achieved remarkable success in various image generation tasks compared with Generative Adversarial Nets (GANs). Recent work on semantic image synthesis mainly follows the de facto GAN-based approaches, which may lead to unsatisfactory quality or diversity of generated images. In this paper, we propose a novel framework based on DDPM for semantic image synthesis. Unlike previous conditional diffusion model directly feeds the semantic layout and noisy image as input to a U-Net structure, which may not fully leverage the information in the input semantic mask, our framework processes semantic layout and noisy image differently. It feeds noisy image to the encoder of the U-Net structure while the semantic layout to the decoder by multi-layer spatially-adaptive normalization operators. To further improve the generation quality and semantic interpretability in semantic image synthesis, we introduce the classifier-free guidance sampling strategy, which acknowledge the scores of an unconditional model for sampling process. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method, achieving state-of-the-art performance in terms of fidelity (FID) and diversity (LPIPS).

item.title: CDFNet: Cross-Dimension Fusion Network with Dual Feature Enhancement for Multimodal Object Detection
item.dc:creator: Wencong Wu
Xiuwei Zhang
Hanlin Yin
Haorui Zeng
Chenxu Wei
Lei Yu
Yanning Zhang
item.prism:publicationName: Expert Systems with Applications
item.description: Multimodal object detection aims to utilize the complementarity between different modalities to improve detection results. However, most existing methods only enhance intermodality features by leveraging the interaction of spatial information while neglecting the interaction of channel information between multimodalities, resulting in insufficient enhancement of cross-modal features. Moreover, many detection models fuse multimodal features within a single feature dimension, failing to consider the use of multi-dimensional information, which means that multimodal feature information has not been fully exploited. To solve these drawbacks, we propose a cross-dimension fusion network with dual feature enhancement (CDFNet) for visible and infrared object detection. Specifically, a dual feature enhancement module (DFEM) is designed to enhance cross-modal representations by modeling multiplicative interactions at both spatial and channel levels. Furthermore, a cross-dimension feature fusion module (CDFFM) is developed for fully integrating the enhanced features by capturing different dimensional dependencies to obtain a more discriminative fused feature. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed CDFNet achieves a 1.8% higher mAP detection accuracy on the LLVIP dataset compared to the state-of-the-art detection method, and exhibits more competitive network complexity than transformer-based and mamba-based models. The code of our CDFNet is released at https://github.com/WenCongWu/CDFNet . Published: 2026-04-07T15:24:19+00:00 Venue: Expert Systems with Applications Score: 0.796 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Wencong Wu; Xiuwei Zhang; Hanlin Yin; Haorui Zeng; Chenxu Wei; Lei Yu; Yanning Zhang

Journal: Expert Systems with Applications

DOI: 10.1016/j.eswa.2026.132380

Relevance: 0.796 (must_read)

Multimodal object detection aims to utilize the complementarity between different modalities to improve detection results. However, most existing methods only enhance intermodality features by leveraging the interaction of spatial information while neglecting the interaction of channel information between multimodalities, resulting in insufficient enhancement of cross-modal features. Moreover, many detection models fuse multimodal features within a single feature dimension, failing to consider the use of multi-dimensional information, which means that multimodal feature information has not been fully exploited. To solve these drawbacks, we propose a cross-dimension fusion network with dual feature enhancement (CDFNet) for visible and infrared object detection. Specifically, a dual feature enhancement module (DFEM) is designed to enhance cross-modal representations by modeling multiplicative interactions at both spatial and channel levels. Furthermore, a cross-dimension feature fusion module (CDFFM) is developed for fully integrating the enhanced features by capturing different dimensional dependencies to obtain a more discriminative fused feature. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed CDFNet achieves a 1.8% higher mAP detection accuracy on the LLVIP dataset compared to the state-of-the-art detection method, and exhibits more competitive network complexity than transformer-based and mamba-based models. The code of our CDFNet is released at https://github.com/WenCongWu/CDFNet .

item.title: Depth-aware sparse queries for efficient multi-view BEV perception
item.dc:creator: Yandong Wu
Chuan Hu
Zhige Chen
Xuewen Cao
Qi Wang
Xiaolin Tang
item.prism:publicationName: Neurocomputing
item.description: The Bird’s-Eye-View (BEV) paradigm has become a foundational framework for multi-view 3D object detection in autonomous driving. However, current sparse query-based methods still struggle to effectively integrate 2D prior information, facing persistent challenges such as inefficient backbone networks, inaccurate depth estimation, unreliable query initialization, and error propagation from 2D detection. To address these challenges, we propose the Depth-Aware Sparse Queries (DASQ) framework. Supported by an efficient backbone network, DASQ first fuses probabilistic and regressed depth estimates to enhance depth accuracy, then converts filtered 2D detections into 3D prior queries, and finally integrates them into the global BEV representation via an attention mechanism. This approach significantly boosts both the accuracy and inference efficiency of BEV perception. In evaluations on the nuScenes benchmark, DASQ achieves a 3.2% improvement in mAP while attaining real-time inference at 10.1 FPS on an NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin platform, demonstrating its strong potential for practical autonomous driving applications. Published: 2026-04-06T16:49:03+00:00 Venue: Neurocomputing Score: 0.795 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yandong Wu; Chuan Hu; Zhige Chen; Xuewen Cao; Qi Wang; Xiaolin Tang

Journal: Neurocomputing

DOI: 10.1016/j.neucom.2026.133585

Relevance: 0.795 (must_read)

The Bird’s-Eye-View (BEV) paradigm has become a foundational framework for multi-view 3D object detection in autonomous driving. However, current sparse query-based methods still struggle to effectively integrate 2D prior information, facing persistent challenges such as inefficient backbone networks, inaccurate depth estimation, unreliable query initialization, and error propagation from 2D detection. To address these challenges, we propose the Depth-Aware Sparse Queries (DASQ) framework. Supported by an efficient backbone network, DASQ first fuses probabilistic and regressed depth estimates to enhance depth accuracy, then converts filtered 2D detections into 3D prior queries, and finally integrates them into the global BEV representation via an attention mechanism. This approach significantly boosts both the accuracy and inference efficiency of BEV perception. In evaluations on the nuScenes benchmark, DASQ achieves a 3.2% improvement in mAP while attaining real-time inference at 10.1 FPS on an NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin platform, demonstrating its strong potential for practical autonomous driving applications.

item.title: HSRD-Net: A Hybrid RemoteSAM-ResNet with Dynamic Weight Assignment for SAR Oil Spill Detection
item.dc:creator: Shen Guo
Ying Li
Zhaoyi Zhang
Peng Wu
Zi Wang
item.prism:publicationName: Expert Systems with Applications
item.description: Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) has become a core data source for marine oil spill detection (OSD) due to its all-weather and day-and-night imaging capabilities. However, the extreme scarcity of publicly available oil spill labels makes deep models highly prone to overfitting in few-shot scenarios, resulting in poor generalization. Meanwhile, the subtle differences in backscatter intensity between oil slicks and look-alikes (such as biological films and low-wind areas) make it difficult for traditional single visual cues or statistical threshold methods to simultaneously achieve complete extraction of ”large-scale, weak-edge” oil slicks and precise suppression of ”small-scale, strong-clutter” false alarms. This leads to high false detection rates and challenges in boundary localization. Therefore, this paper proposes HSRD-Net, a hybrid model architecture based on dynamic weight selection (DWS). It employs RemoteSAM and ResNet as dual encoders to first achieve cross-domain alignment between the scene priors of the large remote sensing model and the transferable representations of the visual pre-trained model. Subsequently, the DWS evaluates the contribution of the two feature streams based on the complexity of the input image and performs adaptive weighting, thereby significantly amplifying weak oil spill scattering signals without introducing additional parameters. Finally, a multi-dimensional loss function (MLF) is used to collaboratively optimize segmentation accuracy and geometric fidelity, distinguishing oil slicks from the background at the pixel level while enhancing edge localization and regional coherence, which significantly suppresses false alarms from look-alike dark spots. Experimental results on two public datasets show that our model consistently sets new state-of-the-art benchmarks, achieving a significant leap compared to existing methods. Published: 2026-04-07T16:25:02+00:00 Venue: Expert Systems with Applications Score: 0.794 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Shen Guo; Ying Li; Zhaoyi Zhang; Peng Wu; Zi Wang

Journal: Expert Systems with Applications

DOI: 10.1016/j.eswa.2026.132384

Relevance: 0.794 (must_read)

Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) has become a core data source for marine oil spill detection (OSD) due to its all-weather and day-and-night imaging capabilities. However, the extreme scarcity of publicly available oil spill labels makes deep models highly prone to overfitting in few-shot scenarios, resulting in poor generalization. Meanwhile, the subtle differences in backscatter intensity between oil slicks and look-alikes (such as biological films and low-wind areas) make it difficult for traditional single visual cues or statistical threshold methods to simultaneously achieve complete extraction of ”large-scale, weak-edge” oil slicks and precise suppression of ”small-scale, strong-clutter” false alarms. This leads to high false detection rates and challenges in boundary localization. Therefore, this paper proposes HSRD-Net, a hybrid model architecture based on dynamic weight selection (DWS). It employs RemoteSAM and ResNet as dual encoders to first achieve cross-domain alignment between the scene priors of the large remote sensing model and the transferable representations of the visual pre-trained model. Subsequently, the DWS evaluates the contribution of the two feature streams based on the complexity of the input image and performs adaptive weighting, thereby significantly amplifying weak oil spill scattering signals without introducing additional parameters. Finally, a multi-dimensional loss function (MLF) is used to collaboratively optimize segmentation accuracy and geometric fidelity, distinguishing oil slicks from the background at the pixel level while enhancing edge localization and regional coherence, which significantly suppresses false alarms from look-alike dark spots. Experimental results on two public datasets show that our model consistently sets new state-of-the-art benchmarks, achieving a significant leap compared to existing methods.

item.title: Robust Adaptation of Foundation Models with Black-Box Visual Prompting
item.dc:creator: Changdae Oh
Gyeongdeok Seo
Geunyoung Jung
Zhi-Qi Cheng
Hosik Choi
Jiyoung Jung
Kyungwoo Song
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: With a surge of large-scale pre-trained models, parameter-efficient transfer learning (PETL) of large models has garnered significant attention. While promising, they commonly rely on two optimistic assumptions: 1) full access to the parameters of a PTM, and 2) sufficient memory capacity to cache all intermediate activations for gradient computation. However, in most real-world applications, PTMs serve as black-box APIs or proprietary software without full parameter accessibility. Besides, it is hard to meet a large memory requirement for modern PTMs. This work proposes black-box visual prompting (BlackVIP), which efficiently adapts the PTMs without knowledge of their architectures or parameters. BlackVIP has two components: 1) Coordinator and 2) simultaneous perturbation stochastic approximation with gradient correction (SPSA-GC). The Coordinator designs input-dependent visual prompts, which allow the target PTM to adapt in the wild. SPSA-GC efficiently estimates the gradient of PTM to update Coordinator. Besides, we introduce a variant, BlackVIP-SE, which significantly reduces the runtime and computational cost of BlackVIP. Extensive experiments on 19 datasets demonstrate that BlackVIPs enable robust adaptation to diverse domains and tasks with minimal memory requirements. We further provide a theoretical analysis on the generalization of visual prompting methods by presenting their connection to the certified robustness of randomized smoothing, and presenting an empirical support for improved robustness. Published: 2026-04-06T19:56:01+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.794 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Changdae Oh; Gyeongdeok Seo; Geunyoung Jung; Zhi-Qi Cheng; Hosik Choi; Jiyoung Jung; Kyungwoo Song

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3681244

Relevance: 0.794 (must_read)

With a surge of large-scale pre-trained models, parameter-efficient transfer learning (PETL) of large models has garnered significant attention. While promising, they commonly rely on two optimistic assumptions: 1) full access to the parameters of a PTM, and 2) sufficient memory capacity to cache all intermediate activations for gradient computation. However, in most real-world applications, PTMs serve as black-box APIs or proprietary software without full parameter accessibility. Besides, it is hard to meet a large memory requirement for modern PTMs. This work proposes black-box visual prompting (BlackVIP), which efficiently adapts the PTMs without knowledge of their architectures or parameters. BlackVIP has two components: 1) Coordinator and 2) simultaneous perturbation stochastic approximation with gradient correction (SPSA-GC). The Coordinator designs input-dependent visual prompts, which allow the target PTM to adapt in the wild. SPSA-GC efficiently estimates the gradient of PTM to update Coordinator. Besides, we introduce a variant, BlackVIP-SE, which significantly reduces the runtime and computational cost of BlackVIP. Extensive experiments on 19 datasets demonstrate that BlackVIPs enable robust adaptation to diverse domains and tasks with minimal memory requirements. We further provide a theoretical analysis on the generalization of visual prompting methods by presenting their connection to the certified robustness of randomized smoothing, and presenting an empirical support for improved robustness.

item.title: Infrared dim and small target detection method based on orthogonal directional saliency and segmented weight assignment
item.dc:creator: Xin Li
Bingjian Wang
Weirong Yang
Dan Wang
Caihui Wang
Jia Li
Huan Li
item.prism:publicationName: Information Sciences
item.description: In complex ground scenarios, infrared dim and small targets often suffer from a low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) due to strong edges and clutter, posing significant detection challenges for Infrared Search and Tracking systems. To address this challenge and meet adaptive detection requirements, this paper develops a saliency enhancement mechanism that is independent of the target SNR, proposing a novel detection method named Orthogonal Directional Saliency and Segmented Weight Assignment (ODSWA). The technique initially employs median-difference and superposition enhancement operations to enhance target-like signatures while suppressing continuous strong edges. Subsequently, a novel three-layer orthogonal Directional window is introduced to compute local contrast. Analysis of small-scale contrast features effectively suppresses adherent local background regions. A segmented weight assignment strategy integrates gray-gradient and variance as dual features to achieve adaptive target discrimination with corresponding saliency weighting. Finally, threshold segmentation is applied to the combined results of local contrast and weight assignment to produce the final output. The key advantage of ODSWA is its ability to maintain target saliency under extreme SNR conditions as low as 0.5. Experimental results confirm that the ODSWA significantly improves target detection capability across varying SNRs in complex environments, demonstrating enhanced adaptability while maintaining high detection accuracy. Published: 2026-04-06T01:07:54+00:00 Venue: Information Sciences Score: 0.794 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xin Li; Bingjian Wang; Weirong Yang; Dan Wang; Caihui Wang; Jia Li; Huan Li

Journal: Information Sciences

DOI: 10.1016/j.ins.2026.123449

Relevance: 0.794 (must_read)

In complex ground scenarios, infrared dim and small targets often suffer from a low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) due to strong edges and clutter, posing significant detection challenges for Infrared Search and Tracking systems. To address this challenge and meet adaptive detection requirements, this paper develops a saliency enhancement mechanism that is independent of the target SNR, proposing a novel detection method named Orthogonal Directional Saliency and Segmented Weight Assignment (ODSWA). The technique initially employs median-difference and superposition enhancement operations to enhance target-like signatures while suppressing continuous strong edges. Subsequently, a novel three-layer orthogonal Directional window is introduced to compute local contrast. Analysis of small-scale contrast features effectively suppresses adherent local background regions. A segmented weight assignment strategy integrates gray-gradient and variance as dual features to achieve adaptive target discrimination with corresponding saliency weighting. Finally, threshold segmentation is applied to the combined results of local contrast and weight assignment to produce the final output. The key advantage of ODSWA is its ability to maintain target saliency under extreme SNR conditions as low as 0.5. Experimental results confirm that the ODSWA significantly improves target detection capability across varying SNRs in complex environments, demonstrating enhanced adaptability while maintaining high detection accuracy.

item.title: DynaPURLS: Dynamic Refinement of Part-Aware Representations for Skeleton-Based Zero-Shot Action Recognition
item.dc:creator: Jingmin Zhu
Anqi Zhu
James Bailey
Jun Liu
Hossein Rahmani
Mohammed Bennamoun
Farid Boussaid
Qiuhong Ke
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Zero-shot skeleton-based action recognition (ZS-SAR) is fundamentally constrained by prevailing approaches that rely on aligning skeleton features with static, class-level semantics. This coarse-grained alignment fails to bridge the domain shift between seen, unseen classes, thereby impeding the effective transfer of fine-grained visual knowledge. To address these limitations, we introduce DynaPURLS, a unified framework that establishes robust, multi-scale visual-semantic correspondences, dynamically refines them at inference time to enhance generalization. Our framework leverages a large language model to generate hierarchical textual descriptions that encompass both global movements, local body-part dynamics. Concurrently, an adaptive partitioning module produces fine-grained visual representations by semantically grouping skeleton joints. To fortify this fine-grained alignment against the train-test domain shift, DynaPURLS incorporates a dynamic refinement module. During inference, this module adapts textual features to the incoming visual stream via a lightweight learnable projection. This refinement process is stabilized by a confidence-aware, class-balanced memory bank, which mitigates error propagation from noisy pseudo-labels. Extensive experiments on three large-scale benchmark datasets, including NTU RGB+D 60/120, PKU-MMD, demonstrate that DynaPURLS significantly outperforms prior art, setting new state-of-the-art records. The source code is made publicly available at https://github.com/Alchemist0754/DynaPURLS Published: 2026-04-06T19:56:01+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.792 (must_read)

Authors: Jingmin Zhu; Anqi Zhu; James Bailey; Jun Liu; Hossein Rahmani; Mohammed Bennamoun; Farid Boussaid; Qiuhong Ke

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3680873

Relevance: 0.792 (must_read)

Zero-shot skeleton-based action recognition (ZS-SAR) is fundamentally constrained by prevailing approaches that rely on aligning skeleton features with static, class-level semantics. This coarse-grained alignment fails to bridge the domain shift between seen, unseen classes, thereby impeding the effective transfer of fine-grained visual knowledge. To address these limitations, we introduce DynaPURLS, a unified framework that establishes robust, multi-scale visual-semantic correspondences, dynamically refines them at inference time to enhance generalization. Our framework leverages a large language model to generate hierarchical textual descriptions that encompass both global movements, local body-part dynamics. Concurrently, an adaptive partitioning module produces fine-grained visual representations by semantically grouping skeleton joints. To fortify this fine-grained alignment against the train-test domain shift, DynaPURLS incorporates a dynamic refinement module. During inference, this module adapts textual features to the incoming visual stream via a lightweight learnable projection. This refinement process is stabilized by a confidence-aware, class-balanced memory bank, which mitigates error propagation from noisy pseudo-labels. Extensive experiments on three large-scale benchmark datasets, including NTU RGB+D 60/120, PKU-MMD, demonstrate that DynaPURLS significantly outperforms prior art, setting new state-of-the-art records. The source code is made publicly available at https://github.com/Alchemist0754/DynaPURLS

item.title: Evidential Robust Feature Learning for Generalized Few-Shot Segmentation
item.dc:creator: Weide Liu
Xiaoyang Zhong
Lu Wang
Chunbo Lang
Yuming Fang
Jun Cheng
Xulei Yang
Gong Cheng
item.prism:publicationName: International Journal of Computer Vision
item.description: Generalized few-shot segmentation aims to recognize novel class objects with limited annotated samples while preserving the ability to segment previously learned base class objects. However, the inadequate training samples make it a challenging task to capture robust features which are both generally transferable for learning novel classes while retaining the capability to discriminate for representing base classes without incurring forgetfulness. To tackle this challenge, we introduce an evidential learning approach that simultaneously improves feature representation robustness and reduces model uncertainty. Specifically, we leverage both general-purpose features from segmentation-centric large pre-trained foundation models and low-level structural features with a physics-informed constraint to improve representations, especially for novel classes. Moreover, we impose our model with the capability of uncertainty estimation by evidential learning. The awareness of the model uncertainty greatly improves the performance in novel classes. The calibrated estimation of model uncertainty, in turn, helps to quantitatively assess the feature robustness for few-shot learning. We evaluate our methods across a diverse range of datasets encompassing natural image datasets, medical image datasets, and remote sensing datasets. The experimental results on the PASCAL VOC, MS COCO, ISIC, ISPRS, and Zurich Summer datasets consistently demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed method and achieve new state-of-the-art. The source code will be made available at https://github.com/liuweide01/ERFL . Published: 2026-04-07T03:14:42+00:00 Venue: International Journal of Computer Vision Score: 0.792 (must_read)

Journal: International Journal of Computer Vision

DOI: 10.1007/s11263-026-02796-1

Relevance: 0.792 (must_read)

Generalized few-shot segmentation aims to recognize novel class objects with limited annotated samples while preserving the ability to segment previously learned base class objects. However, the inadequate training samples make it a challenging task to capture robust features which are both generally transferable for learning novel classes while retaining the capability to discriminate for representing base classes without incurring forgetfulness. To tackle this challenge, we introduce an evidential learning approach that simultaneously improves feature representation robustness and reduces model uncertainty. Specifically, we leverage both general-purpose features from segmentation-centric large pre-trained foundation models and low-level structural features with a physics-informed constraint to improve representations, especially for novel classes. Moreover, we impose our model with the capability of uncertainty estimation by evidential learning. The awareness of the model uncertainty greatly improves the performance in novel classes. The calibrated estimation of model uncertainty, in turn, helps to quantitatively assess the feature robustness for few-shot learning. We evaluate our methods across a diverse range of datasets encompassing natural image datasets, medical image datasets, and remote sensing datasets. The experimental results on the PASCAL VOC, MS COCO, ISIC, ISPRS, and Zurich Summer datasets consistently demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed method and achieve new state-of-the-art. The source code will be made available at https://github.com/liuweide01/ERFL .

item.title: MOFM: A Multiple-in-One Flow Mamba for Unregistered Multi-Modal Image Fusion
item.dc:creator: Bo Yang
Zhaohui Jiang
Dong Pan
Zhiping Lin
Weihua Gui
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: Multi-modal image fusion aims to integrate complementary cues from different modalities into a single image, facilitating downstream tasks such as object detection. However, input image pairs are often misaligned due to rigid or non-rigid deformations in practical scenarios. Such misregistration introduces structural distortions and visual artifacts, reducing the reliability of the fused results and limiting their effectiveness for downstream vision applications. While existing methods demonstrate satisfactory results under specific deformation scenarios, they exhibit limited generalization to diverse and severe misregistrations. To this end, this study proposes a unified multiple-in-one flow Mamba framework for registering various image deformations and generating high-quality fused results. Specifically, a hierarchical flow Mamba is designed to model rigid and non-rigid flow fields and enhance adaptability to complex deformations by progressively refining misaligned features. To better distinguish between rigid and non-rigid types, a flow field classifier predicts rigid/non-rigid categories and provides prompts for high-level feature modulation. Furthermore, a flow-guided fusion Mamba module is developed to aggregate aligned multi-level modality features and generate fused images, while an iterative training strategy enables collaborative optimization by using fusion outputs to refine flow estimation. Experiments across three representative modality tasks demonstrate that the proposed method delivers superior fusion performance while maintaining applicability to object detection. The code will be available at: https://github.com/BOYang-pro/MOFM. Published: 2026-04-07T20:02:42+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.791 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Bo Yang; Zhaohui Jiang; Dong Pan; Zhiping Lin; Weihua Gui

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3681540

Relevance: 0.791 (must_read)

Multi-modal image fusion aims to integrate complementary cues from different modalities into a single image, facilitating downstream tasks such as object detection. However, input image pairs are often misaligned due to rigid or non-rigid deformations in practical scenarios. Such misregistration introduces structural distortions and visual artifacts, reducing the reliability of the fused results and limiting their effectiveness for downstream vision applications. While existing methods demonstrate satisfactory results under specific deformation scenarios, they exhibit limited generalization to diverse and severe misregistrations. To this end, this study proposes a unified multiple-in-one flow Mamba framework for registering various image deformations and generating high-quality fused results. Specifically, a hierarchical flow Mamba is designed to model rigid and non-rigid flow fields and enhance adaptability to complex deformations by progressively refining misaligned features. To better distinguish between rigid and non-rigid types, a flow field classifier predicts rigid/non-rigid categories and provides prompts for high-level feature modulation. Furthermore, a flow-guided fusion Mamba module is developed to aggregate aligned multi-level modality features and generate fused images, while an iterative training strategy enables collaborative optimization by using fusion outputs to refine flow estimation. Experiments across three representative modality tasks demonstrate that the proposed method delivers superior fusion performance while maintaining applicability to object detection. The code will be available at: https://github.com/BOYang-pro/MOFM.

item.title: RT-DETR-AMRS: Lightweight Real-Time Transformer Detection Framework for Small Remote Sensing Targets
item.dc:creator: Hui Wang
Shilei Zhao
Anhong Zhou
Zijun Liu
Yuhang Bian
Ruixue Xiao
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
item.description: Accurately detecting tiny, densely packed objects in remote-sensing imagery remains a formidable challenge due to severe feature vanishing and complex background clutter. In this work, we present RT-DETR-AMRS, a lightweight re-design of the Real-Time Detection Transformer (RT-DETR) specifically tailored for aerial small-object scenarios. Our approach integrates three remote-sensing–oriented components: C3k2-MCAttn, a dual-path residual block equipped with Monte-Carlo attention to enrich fine-scale cues; C2f-CAMixer, a lightweight channel–spatial mixer that strengthens local feature fusion without increasing architectural complexity; and DySample, a learnable, resolution-aware up-sampler that preserves spatial fidelity far better than naïve interpolation. To rigorously benchmark these upgrades, we introduce TinyRS, a challenging dataset of 20,000 images comprising four representative aerial classes captured under densely cluttered conditions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RT-DETR-AMRS achieves 82.5% mAP50 on TinyRS, outperforming the RT-DETR-R18 baseline by 2.5 pp and surpassing state-of-the-art YOLO variants. Crucially, our model reduces parameters by 32% and FLOPs by 21%, while exhibiting superior generalization on public benchmarks such as DOTA-v2.0 and VisDrone2019, striking an optimal balance between detection accuracy and real-time efficiency. Published: 2026-04-07T20:03:46+00:00 Venue: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters Score: 0.789 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Hui Wang; Shilei Zhao; Anhong Zhou; Zijun Liu; Yuhang Bian; Ruixue Xiao

Journal: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters

DOI: 10.1109/lgrs.2026.3681702

Relevance: 0.789 (must_read)

Accurately detecting tiny, densely packed objects in remote-sensing imagery remains a formidable challenge due to severe feature vanishing and complex background clutter. In this work, we present RT-DETR-AMRS, a lightweight re-design of the Real-Time Detection Transformer (RT-DETR) specifically tailored for aerial small-object scenarios. Our approach integrates three remote-sensing–oriented components: C3k2-MCAttn, a dual-path residual block equipped with Monte-Carlo attention to enrich fine-scale cues; C2f-CAMixer, a lightweight channel–spatial mixer that strengthens local feature fusion without increasing architectural complexity; and DySample, a learnable, resolution-aware up-sampler that preserves spatial fidelity far better than naïve interpolation. To rigorously benchmark these upgrades, we introduce TinyRS, a challenging dataset of 20,000 images comprising four representative aerial classes captured under densely cluttered conditions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RT-DETR-AMRS achieves 82.5% mAP50 on TinyRS, outperforming the RT-DETR-R18 baseline by 2.5 pp and surpassing state-of-the-art YOLO variants. Crucially, our model reduces parameters by 32% and FLOPs by 21%, while exhibiting superior generalization on public benchmarks such as DOTA-v2.0 and VisDrone2019, striking an optimal balance between detection accuracy and real-time efficiency.
